assertions.expectation: Defines 'available' as versions officially available in the channel that customers can manually upgrade to
assertions.reason: Response defines available as 'the oldest supported version in the current release channel' and 'the minimum version you can manually select when creating new clusters or node pools'

assertions.expectation: Defines 'default' as the version used for new cluster creation — typically the same as auto-upgrade target but can differ especially during new minor version introduction (separate promotion stage)
assertions.reason: Response defines default correctly as what new clusters get by default, but doesn't explain the relationship with auto-upgrade target or mention separate promotion stages during minor version rollouts

assertions.expectation: Defines 'auto-upgrade target' as the version GKE will actually upgrade existing clusters to automatically — this is cluster-specific based on policies
assertions.reason: Response correctly defines auto-upgrade target as 'what your specific cluster will be upgraded to' and mentions it's 'determined based on your cluster's current version and upgrade path'

assertions.expectation: Explains that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same but CAN differ, especially during new minor version rollouts
assertions.reason: Response shows they are different in the example but doesn't explicitly explain that they are often the same or why they can differ during minor version rollouts

assertions.expectation: Notes that the auto-upgrade target depends on cluster constraints (maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions) and is cluster-specific — different clusters on the same channel can have different targets
assertions.reason: Response mentions it's cluster-specific but doesn't explain maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions, or that different clusters on same channel can have different targets

assertions.expectation: Explains that a cluster with a 'no minor' exclusion will have a patch-only auto-upgrade target for its current minor
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or 'no minor' exclusion policies at all

assertions.expectation: Recommends using gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info to check the actual auto-upgrade status, target versions, and EoS timestamps
assertions.reason: Response does not mention any gcloud commands or tools for checking upgrade information

assertions.expectation: Points to the GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
assertions.reason: Response does not reference GKE release schedule, release notes, or any documentation resources
